1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: What did Moab do after the death of Ahab?
questions.explanation: Moab rebelled against Israel after Ahab's death.

questions.question: What happened to Ahaziah that caused him to seek Baal-zebub?

options.B: He fell through a lattice and became sick
options.D: He was cursed by a prophet

questions.explanation: Ahaziah fell through a lattice in his upper chamber and sought the god of Ekron.

questions.question: Who was sent by the LORD to meet Ahaziah's messengers?
questions.explanation: The angel of the LORD sent Elijah to intercept the messengers.

questions.question: How was Elijah described by those who saw him?

options.A: A hairy man girded with a leather girdle
options.B: A young man in white robes
options.C: An old man with a staff

questions.explanation: Elijah was identified by his distinctive appearance.

questions.question: What happened when the first captain of fifty came to Elijah?

options.A: He persuaded Elijah to come down
options.D: Fire came down from heaven and consumed him and his men

questions.explanation: Elijah called down fire from heaven in judgment.

questions.question: What was different about the third captain of fifty?

options.B: He fell on his knees and begged for mercy
options.C: He was stronger than the others

questions.explanation: The third captain humbled himself and appealed to Elijah's compassion.

questions.question: Who became king after Ahaziah's death?
questions.explanation: Jehoram, son of Jehoshaphat, took the throne as Ahaziah had no son.

questions.question: What was about to happen to Elijah?

options.A: The LORD was taking him up into heaven by a whirlwind
options.B: He was going to rest in the land
options.D: He was about to be crowned king

questions.explanation: Elijah's time on earth was ending as God would take him to heaven.

questions.question: What did Elisha promise Elijah as they traveled?
questions.explanation: Elisha vowed to stay with Elijah throughout his departure.

questions.question: What did Elisha ask of Elijah before he was taken away?

options.C: A double portion of his spirit

questions.explanation: Elisha requested that he inherit twice the spiritual power of Elijah.

questions.question: How was Elijah taken from the earth?

options.B: A chariot of fire and horses of fire came, and he went up by a whirlwind
options.C: He sailed away on the sea
options.D: An angel carried him to heaven

questions.explanation: Elijah was taken up in a whirlwind with a chariot and horses of fire.

questions.question: What did Elisha do when he saw Elijah taken up?

options.A: Cried out and tore his clothes

questions.explanation: Elisha cried 'My father, my father, the chariot of Israel' and rent his clothes.

questions.question: What did Elisha pick up after Elijah was taken?

options.D: Elijah's mantle that fell from him

questions.explanation: Elisha took up the mantle of Elijah as a symbol of his prophetic succession.

questions.question: How did Elisha prove he had received Elijah's spirit?

options.B: He struck the waters of Jordan with the mantle, and they parted

questions.explanation: The parting of the Jordan showed that Elijah's spirit rested upon Elisha.

questions.question: What problem did the men of the city tell Elisha about their situation?

options.C: The water was naught and the ground was barren

questions.explanation: The water and land had become unusable for the city.

questions.question: How did Elisha heal the waters?

options.A: He cast salt into the spring and spoke the LORD's word
options.C: He brought water from another city

questions.explanation: Elisha used salt as a symbol of healing when speaking God's word.

questions.question: What did the little children do to Elisha as he went up toward Beth-el?

options.B: They mocked him, saying 'Go up, thou bald head'

questions.explanation: The children taunted Elisha about his baldness.

questions.question: What was the consequence of the children mocking Elisha?

options.D: Two she bears came out of the wood and tore forty-two of them

questions.explanation: Elisha cursed them, and bears came and mauled the mocking children.

questions.question: How long did Jehoram reign over Israel?
questions.explanation: Jehoram reigned for twelve years over Israel.

questions.question: What did Jehoram do that was different from his father Ahab?

options.C: He removed the image of Baal that his father had made

questions.explanation: Jehoram removed the Baal image, though he still sinned in other ways.

questions.question: Who was Mesha and what was his occupation?

options.A: King of Moab and a sheepmaster

questions.explanation: Mesha was the king of Moab who raised sheep and paid tribute to Israel.

questions.question: What did King Jehoram ask Jehoshaphat to do?

options.B: Go with him against Moab in battle

questions.explanation: Jehoram requested that Jehoshaphat join him in war against the rebelling Moabites.

questions.question: What problem did the three kings face on their journey?

options.B: They got lost in the desert
options.D: There was no water for the host and animals

questions.explanation: A water shortage threatened the armies after seven days of travel.

questions.question: Who did Jehoshaphat suggest they consult?
questions.explanation: Jehoshaphat asked for a prophet of the LORD to inquire of God.

questions.question: Why was Elisha willing to help despite the king of Israel's sins?

options.B: He was afraid of the king
options.C: He regarded the presence of Jehoshaphat king of Judah

questions.explanation: Elisha respected Jehoshaphat and would help for his sake.

questions.question: What did Elisha command the kings to make?
questions.explanation: Elisha told them to dig ditches throughout the valley.

questions.question: What would happen to the valley of ditches?

options.A: It would be filled with water without wind or rain

questions.explanation: The LORD would fill the ditches with water miraculously.

questions.question: What did the Moabites think when they saw the water?

options.A: It was a blessing from their god
options.C: It was blood, and that the kings had slain one another
options.D: It was a test of their faith

questions.explanation: The Moabites mistook the red-colored water for blood.

questions.question: What happened when the Moabites came to the camp of Israel?

options.B: The Israelites rose up and smote them, driving them back

questions.explanation: The Israelites defeated the Moabites decisively.

questions.question: Why did a widow of the sons of the prophets cry to Elisha?

options.C: She wanted to become a prophet
options.D: Her husband was dead and creditors would take her sons as bondmen

questions.explanation: The widow faced losing her sons to debt slavery after her husband's death.

questions.question: What did the widow have in her house?
questions.explanation: She possessed only a small pot of oil.

questions.question: What did Elisha instruct the widow to do?

options.D: Go to the king for help

questions.explanation: Elisha told her to gather as many empty vessels as she could from neighbors.

questions.question: What happened when the widow's vessels were full?

options.C: The oil stayed and would not flow anymore

questions.explanation: The miracle stopped when all available vessels were filled.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell the widow to do with the oil?

options.B: Sell it to pay her debt and live on the rest

questions.explanation: She was to sell the oil for income to pay off her debts.

questions.question: What did the Shunammite woman do when Elisha passed by?
questions.explanation: The Shunammite woman insisted on feeding Elisha whenever he passed.

questions.question: What did the Shunammite woman and her husband prepare for Elisha?

options.B: A small chamber on the wall with a bed, table, stool, and candlestick
options.C: A guest house in the city

questions.explanation: They made a simple but complete room dedicated to the prophet's needs.

questions.question: What reward was Elisha prepared to offer the Shunammite woman?

options.A: He offered to speak for her to the king or captain of the host
options.B: He offered to give her money
options.C: He offered to make her a prophetess
options.D: He offered to build her a house

questions.explanation: Elisha offered to use his influence with the king on her behalf.

questions.question: What did Gehazi tell Elisha the Shunammite woman lacked?

options.C: A child, and her husband was old

questions.explanation: Her greatest lack was a son, which would be meaningful in her old age.

questions.question: What did Elisha promise the Shunammite woman?

options.B: About this season according to the time of life, she would embrace a son
options.C: She would travel to distant lands

questions.explanation: Elisha prophesied that she would have a son within a year.

questions.question: Who was Naaman?

options.A: Captain of the host of the king of Syria and a mighty man of valor

questions.explanation: Naaman was a powerful military commander in Syria.

questions.question: What disease did Naaman have?
questions.explanation: Naaman was a leper.

questions.question: What did the Israeli maid tell Naaman's wife?

options.B: That the prophet Elisha could heal his leprosy
options.C: That he should go to Egypt

questions.explanation: The maid spoke of Elisha's power to heal.

questions.question: What did the king of Syria send with Naaman to the king of Israel?

options.C: A letter asking the king of Israel to heal Naaman of his leprosy

questions.explanation: The Syrian king sent a formal letter requesting healing.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell Naaman to do to be cleansed?

options.A: Go and wash in the Jordan seven times
options.C: Make an offering to the temple

questions.explanation: Elisha directed him to wash seven times in the Jordan River.

questions.question: How did Naaman initially react to Elisha's instruction?

options.B: He was wroth and went away, expecting he would do something greater

questions.explanation: Naaman was angry and skeptical about washing in a foreign river.

questions.question: What rivers did Naaman think were better than the Jordan?

options.D: The Red Sea and the Sea of Galilee

questions.explanation: Naaman preferred the rivers of Damascus to the Jordan.

questions.question: What happened after Naaman washed in the Jordan seven times?

options.D: His flesh became like the flesh of a little child and he was clean

questions.explanation: Naaman was completely healed of his leprosy.

questions.question: What did Naaman do after being healed?

options.B: Returned to Elisha and declared that now he knew there was no God in all the earth but in Israel
options.C: Went back to Syria without speaking

questions.explanation: Naaman returned to Elisha to acknowledge God's power.

questions.question: What did Naaman offer to Elisha for his healing?
questions.explanation: Naaman offered to give Elisha a reward for the healing.

questions.question: What happened to Gehazi for his dishonesty?

options.C: The leprosy of Naaman cleaved unto him and his seed forever

questions.explanation: Gehazi's greed and deception resulted in him becoming a leper.

questions.question: Why did Ahaziah turn to Baal-zebub instead of the LORD?

options.A: He wanted to honor multiple gods
options.B: He did not recognize or respect the God of Israel and trusted in foreign deities
options.C: He had made a vow to Baal

questions.explanation: Ahaziah's idolatry showed his rejection of the LORD.

questions.question: What does Elijah's calling down fire reveal about his authority?

options.A: He was merely a political rebel
options.B: He acted alone without God's direction
options.C: He wielded divine power and was God's true representative against the king's false authority
options.D: He was showing off his power

questions.explanation: Elijah's supernatural authority validated his prophetic mission.

questions.question: Why did Elisha's constant refusal to leave Elijah show his character?

options.A: His loyalty and devotion to his mentor and the call of God
options.B: He wanted to be left to rule alone
options.C: He was afraid to be alone
options.D: He did not understand what was happening

questions.explanation: Elisha's persistence demonstrated his deep commitment to Elijah and his spiritual calling.

questions.question: Why did Elisha ask for a double portion of Elijah's spirit?

options.B: He understood the prophetic mantle would pass to him and wanted sufficient spiritual authority for the task
options.C: He wanted to be greater than Elijah

questions.explanation: Elisha recognized the enormous responsibility ahead as Elijah's successor.

questions.question: What does Elijah's ascension suggest about God's power?

options.C: God controls life, death, heaven, and earth; He transcends normal mortality

questions.explanation: Elijah's departure affirmed God's dominion over all creation.

questions.question: Why was Elisha's successful parting of the Jordan significant?

options.A: It confirmed that the prophetic mantle had truly passed from Elijah to Elisha
options.B: It showed the Jordan was not very deep
options.C: It was the only miracle Elisha would perform

questions.explanation: The identical miracle to what Elijah performed validated Elisha's prophetic succession.

questions.question: What does Elisha's healing of the waters reveal about the nature of prophetic ministry?

options.B: The prophet's word, combined with symbolic action and God's power, brings healing and restoration
options.D: Prophets were primarily concerned with natural resources

questions.explanation: Elisha's words and actions, empowered by God, transformed the land.

questions.question: Why was the mocking of Elisha so serious that it warranted such severe judgment?

options.C: It was disrespect for God's prophet, which was fundamentally rebellion against God's authority

questions.explanation: Mockery of the prophet was considered contempt for God Himself.

questions.question: Why did Jehoram removing the image of Baal represent incomplete reform?

options.A: He removed one idol but continued practicing the other sins of Jeroboam
options.B: He built a new one immediately after

questions.explanation: Jehoram's partial obedience did not restore true faithfulness to God.

questions.question: What does the water shortage suggest about divine discipline?

options.B: God tests His people through hardship to prove their faith and draw them to seek Him
options.D: The kings deserved to be punished

questions.explanation: The shortage created the circumstances for the kings to seek Elisha's counsel.

questions.question: Why did Elisha instruct them to make ditches?

options.C: To prepare the valley to receive the water God would provide

questions.explanation: The ditches demonstrated faith and prepared them to receive God's provision.

questions.question: Why did the Moabites' mistaken belief about the water lead to their defeat?

options.A: Their false assumption caused them to rush into battle unprepared, enabling an easy Israelite victory

questions.explanation: The Moabites' error in perception directly caused their military downfall.

questions.question: Why was the widow's plight a matter of justice for Elisha?

options.B: She was vulnerable; her late husband had feared the LORD, and she and her sons faced unjust servitude

questions.explanation: Elisha's intervention showed God's concern for the vulnerable and oppressed.

questions.question: Why did Elisha instruct her to borrow vessels from neighbors?

options.C: To demonstrate her faith and prepare vessels to receive the miracle God would provide
options.D: To get to know her neighbors

questions.explanation: The borrowing was an act of faith; God would fill them.

questions.question: Why did the oil stop flowing when the vessels were full?

options.A: The widow ran out of oil
options.B: The miracle demonstrated the exact measure of God's provision; no excess, but exactly what was needed

questions.explanation: God's provision was perfectly calibrated to her need and faith.

questions.question: What does the Shunammite woman's hospitality reveal about her spiritual understanding?

options.A: She recognized that caring for God's prophet was a spiritual privilege and duty
options.D: She had nothing else to do

questions.explanation: Her generosity reflected reverence for God's servant.

questions.question: Why was the promise of a son particularly meaningful to the Shunammite woman?

options.C: In her culture and age, a son ensured her security and continued her family line

questions.explanation: A son represented hope, legacy, and security for an older woman.

questions.question: Why is Naaman described as both mighty and leprous?

options.B: To show that greatness and power in one area cannot protect against spiritual or physical ailment; all need God

questions.explanation: Naaman's leprosy humbled his pride despite his military status.

questions.question: What was remarkable about the Israeli maid's faith?

options.A: Though a slave in an enemy land, she trusted in Israel's God and spoke boldly
options.D: She had no faith at all

questions.explanation: The maid's testimony became the catalyst for Naaman's healing.

questions.question: Why did Naaman's initial resistance to Elisha's instruction show pride?

options.C: He expected a dramatic performance befitting his status, not a simple washing in a foreign river

questions.explanation: Naaman wanted a cure worthy of his importance, not simple obedience.

questions.question: How did Naaman's opinion of his home rivers show limited understanding?

options.B: He valued natural advantage over obedience to God's word; the Jordan's supposed inferiority was irrelevant

questions.explanation: Naaman's faith had to overcome his cultural prejudices.

questions.question: How did Naaman's servants help him overcome his pride?

options.A: They appealed to logic: if Elisha had asked something great, he would have done it; why not obey something simple?

questions.explanation: The servants' wisdom turned Naaman toward obedience.

questions.question: Why was Naaman's seven-fold washing significant?

options.C: Seven represents completion and perfection; the full healing required complete obedience

questions.explanation: The repetition symbolized total transformation and trust.

questions.question: What transformation occurred in Naaman's understanding of God?

options.B: He moved from skepticism to conviction that Israel's God is the only true God

questions.explanation: Naaman's healing produced theological conversion, not just physical cure.

questions.question: Why did Gehazi's deception compound his sin?

options.A: He lied to Elisha, who had performed a miraculous healing, showing ingratitude and greed

questions.explanation: Gehazi's lie directly opposed Elisha's generosity and God's grace.

questions.question: What does Ahaziah's choice to seek Baal-zebub demonstrate about spiritual blindness?

options.A: Even in crisis, those who reject God turn to false authorities rather than seek Him

questions.explanation: Ahaziah's instinctive turn to idolatry revealed the hardness of his heart.

questions.question: How did the fire judgments on the captains demonstrate both God's power and mercy?

options.B: The first two captains were destroyed for defying Elijah, but the third, who humbled himself, was spared; this shows judgment on pride but mercy on repentance

questions.explanation: God's judgment differentiated based on the response to His prophet.

questions.question: Why did Elisha's request for a double portion place him in a position of greater responsibility?

options.C: Greater spiritual authority demands greater faithfulness; Elisha would need double Elijah's power to face the coming spiritual challenges in Israel

questions.explanation: Elisha's request acknowledged that his successor role would be demanding.

questions.question: What does Elijah's ascension teach about God's sovereignty over life, death, and eternity?

options.B: God transcends the limitations of mortality; He can reverse death and transport persons to heaven, demonstrating His absolute authority

questions.explanation: Elijah's translation affirmed that God rules all dimensions of existence.

questions.question: Why was Elisha's successful replication of the Jordan parting not merely a repetition?

options.A: It established him as Elijah's legitimate successor and proved the prophetic mantle had genuinely transferred

questions.explanation: The people recognized in this miracle the validation of Elisha's call.

questions.question: How did Elisha's healing of the barren land foreshadow his broader ministry?

options.A: He would only work miracles of water
options.C: As a prophet, he would bring spiritual restoration and healing to individuals and the land, reversing the effects of curse and sin

questions.explanation: The healing mirrored Elisha's restorative prophetic calling throughout his ministry.

questions.question: Why does the episode of the mocking children reveal a principle about honor and authority?

options.B: Dishonoring God's representative is tantamount to dishonoring God; mockery of authority reflects rejection of ultimate divine authority

questions.explanation: The severity of the judgment showed the seriousness of disrespecting God's prophet.

questions.question: How does Jehoram's partial reform illustrate the inadequacy of superficial obedience?

options.A: Removing one idol while continuing idolatry shows that piecemeal obedience does not substitute for whole-hearted faithfulness to God
options.D: One act of obedience is enough

questions.explanation: True repentance requires comprehensive turning from sin.

questions.question: What theological principle does the water shortage illustrate?

options.C: Hardship can be a disciplinary tool; through crisis, even stubborn rulers are brought to seek God's wisdom
options.D: God does not care about kings

questions.explanation: The shortage created opportunity for spiritual conversion.

questions.question: How did Elisha's statement that bringing victory was 'a light thing in the sight of the LORD' reveal God's supremacy?

options.B: What humans consider great military and political victories are small matters for God; His power transcends all human limitation

questions.explanation: Elisha's statement put human concerns in perspective of God's infinite power.

questions.question: Why was the widow's case more than a personal hardship; what broader principle does it address?

options.A: Prophetic ministry extends to defending the vulnerable from exploitation; God's concern for justice includes protecting widows and children from abuse

questions.explanation: The passage illustrates God's protective concern for the defenseless.

questions.question: Why did Elisha's instruction to borrow vessels test and develop the widow's faith?

options.C: She had to publicly commit to the miracle's possibility before it occurred; faith must precede the blessing

questions.explanation: The act of borrowing vessels was a public declaration of faith in God's provision.

questions.question: How did the exact cessation of the oil reflect God's character?

options.B: God is neither profligate nor stingy; His provision is precisely calibrated to need and accomplishes His purposes with neither waste nor shortage

questions.explanation: The precise amount reflected God's perfect knowledge and care.

questions.question: How did the Shunammite woman's hospitality foreshadow the nature of prophetic relationship?

options.A: True spiritual relationship involves sacrifice and service; honoring God's word carriers leads to personal blessing

questions.explanation: Her generosity created the relational foundation for her receiving the promise of a son.

questions.question: Why was the birth of the promised son deeply ironic given the Shunammite woman's earlier claim?

options.C: She told Elisha she dwelt among her own people and needed nothing, yet Elisha's knowledge of her real need surpassed her own self-awareness
options.D: The son was not her own

questions.explanation: God's grace transcends human claims of self-sufficiency.

questions.question: Why was Naaman's greatness in war contrasted with his powerlessness against leprosy?

options.B: Human achievement and power cannot deliver us from the fundamental condition of need; all must ultimately depend on God

questions.explanation: Naaman's leprosy leveled him despite his military status, showing all need God.

questions.question: What does the Israeli maid's testimony reveal about evangelism in difficult circumstances?

options.A: Even in captivity, witnesses can testify to God's power; faith is contagious and spreads through simple testimony

questions.explanation: The maid's simple faith became the channel through which God worked Naaman's healing.

questions.question: How does Naaman's expectation of a grandiose healing method reflect spiritual pride?

options.C: Naaman believed his importance demanded extraordinary treatment; his pride expected a healing worthy of his status, not the simple obedience to God's instruction

questions.explanation: Naaman's pride initially obscured the simple path to healing.

questions.question: Why did Naaman's prejudice about the Jordan nearly prevented his healing?

options.A: The Jordan was not good water
options.B: Prejudice and cultural pride can blind us to God's provision; our preconceptions can prevent us from receiving grace

questions.explanation: Naaman had to overcome his cultural assumptions to obey God.

questions.question: Why were Naaman's servants more spiritually perceptive than their master?

options.A: Humility opens the heart to wisdom; those without pride can see truth; servants perhaps lacked Naaman's status-consciousness

questions.explanation: The servants' lack of pride enabled them to recognize the logic of faith.

questions.question: How did the number seven in Naaman's seven-fold washing carry symbolic weight?

options.C: Seven represents completion and wholeness; Naaman's full healing required full obedience, which is symbolized in the complete action

questions.explanation: The sevenfold action symbolized complete transformation.

questions.question: Why did Naaman's confession that Israel's God is the only God represent genuine conversion?

options.B: True conversion aligns the intellect, will, and heart with truth; Naaman moved from skepticism to conviction to public acknowledgment of God's uniqueness

questions.explanation: Naaman's statement reflected genuine spiritual transformation.

questions.question: Why did Gehazi's pursuit of Naaman's gifts represent a fundamental misunderstanding of prophetic calling?

options.A: Prophetic ministry is about serving God and others, not personal enrichment; Gehazi's greed betrayed the very principles his master represented

questions.explanation: Gehazi's greed contradicted the prophetic calling to serve God, not self.

questions.question: Why was Gehazi's leprosy a form of poetic justice?

options.C: Gehazi wanted to acquire Naaman's substance through deception; instead, he acquired Naaman's disease, showing God's justice is exact and reverses ill-gotten gain

questions.explanation: The judgment mirrored and negated Gehazi's crime precisely.

questions.question: What did the sons of the prophets ask Elisha?

options.B: To allow them to build a larger dwelling place
options.C: To teach them how to perform miracles

questions.explanation: The prophetic school needed more space due to growth.

questions.question: What happened to one of the sons of the prophets while cutting timber?

options.B: He fell out of a tree
options.C: The iron axe head fell into the water and was lost

questions.explanation: The axe head sank in the water during the construction work.

questions.question: Why was the loss of the axe head particularly serious?

options.A: It was borrowed and the man was afraid of the consequence
options.D: The man would be punished immediately

questions.explanation: The borrowed tool created a debt and responsibility.

questions.question: How did Elisha recover the axe head?

options.A: He dove into the water and retrieved it
options.C: He asked the man to search
options.D: He cut down a stick, cast it into the water, and the iron did swim

questions.explanation: Elisha's miraculous action made the iron float, defying natural law.

questions.question: How did the king of Syria learn that Elisha was revealing his military plans?

options.B: His servants told him that Elisha knew his secret counsel

questions.explanation: The king heard reports of Elisha's supernatural knowledge.

questions.question: What did the king of Syria decide to do about Elisha?
questions.explanation: The king saw Elisha as a threat and wanted to capture him.

questions.question: What was the servant of Elisha afraid of when he saw the Syrian army?

options.C: They were surrounded with horses and chariots

questions.explanation: The servant saw the overwhelming military force surrounding them.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell his servant about those with them?

options.B: Those that be with us are more than those that be with them

questions.explanation: Elisha assured him that God's forces vastly outnumbered the visible enemy.

questions.question: What did Elisha pray for regarding his servant?

options.A: That the LORD would open his eyes so he could see the mountain full of horses and chariots of fire

questions.explanation: Elisha prayed for spiritual sight to perceive God's protection.

questions.question: What happened when the Syrian army came down to Elisha?

options.D: Elisha prayed that the LORD would smite them with blindness

questions.explanation: God blinded the Syrian soldiers in response to Elisha's prayer.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell the blinded Syrian army?

options.A: This is the way to Syria
options.B: Follow me and I will take you to the king
options.C: This is not the way, neither is this the city; follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom ye seek

questions.explanation: Elisha led them with deception toward Samaria.

questions.question: What happened when the Syrians' eyes were opened?

options.B: They found themselves in the midst of Samaria, surrounded
options.D: They attacked the king of Israel

questions.explanation: The Syrians realized they had been captured.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell the king of Israel NOT to do with the captured Syrians?
questions.explanation: Elisha counseled mercy instead of violence.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell the king TO do with the captured Syrians?

options.D: Set bread and water before them that they may eat and drink and go to their master

questions.explanation: Elisha advocated feeding and releasing them instead of killing them.

questions.question: What was the result of Elisha's mercy toward the Syrians?

options.C: The bands of Syrians came no more into the land of Israel
options.D: They captured the king of Israel

questions.explanation: The Syrians ceased their raiding because of the kindness shown.

questions.question: What did Elisha prophesy during the famine in Samaria?

options.B: About this time tomorrow a measure of fine flour shall be sold for a shekel

questions.explanation: Elisha prophesied that food would suddenly become abundant.

questions.question: Who sat at the entering in of the gate of Samaria?
questions.explanation: Four leprous men were excluded from the city during the famine.

questions.question: What did the lepers reason among themselves?

options.A: To go to the temple for healing
options.D: To go to the Syrian camp, for if they stay in the city they will die of famine; if they go to the Syrians they might die or be spared

questions.explanation: The lepers reasoned that they had nothing to lose by going to the enemy camp.

questions.question: What did the lepers find when they came to the Syrian camp?

options.C: No one there; the Syrians had fled, leaving food, vessels, and treasures

questions.explanation: The camp had been mysteriously abandoned with all provisions intact.

questions.question: Why had the Syrians fled the camp?

options.A: They saw the Israelite army approaching
options.B: The LORD had caused them to hear a noise of chariots and horses and a great host, so they thought a larger army was coming

questions.explanation: God created a supernatural noise that frightened them into fleeing.

questions.question: What did the lepers decide to do after gathering the treasures?

options.A: Go and tell the king's household

questions.explanation: The lepers realized they should share the good news with the city.

questions.question: Why was Elisha's recovery of the axe head significant beyond the surface miracle?

options.A: It demonstrated a minor magic trick
options.B: It showed Elisha's concern for even small problems; he valued the worry and responsibility of a humble student
options.D: It only mattered because the axe was valuable

questions.explanation: Elisha's intervention showed his pastoral care extended to all needs.

questions.question: Why was the king of Syria's discovery of Elisha's role threatening to his military strategy?

options.C: Elisha's prophetic knowledge neutralized the Syrians' military advantage; he could warn Israel of every ambush

questions.explanation: Prophetic intelligence made Israel invulnerable to surprise attacks.

questions.question: What did the servant's fear reveal about the limits of natural perception?

options.A: Natural sight alone, without faith, produces fear; spiritual reality transcends what the eyes can see

questions.explanation: The servant's fear showed how limited physical perception can be.

questions.question: What does Elisha's prayer for his servant's spiritual sight teach about faith?

options.A: Only special people can see God's protection
options.B: Faith requires spiritual perception; opening our eyes to God's reality dispels fear and produces trust
options.D: We do not need to pray

questions.explanation: Spiritual awareness brings peace in the midst of danger.

questions.question: Why was God's blinding of the Syrian army more merciful than killing them?

options.C: It allowed Elisha to lead them to safety and the king to show them mercy; the blindness protected both them and Israel from bloodshed

questions.explanation: The blindness created an opportunity for mercy instead of conflict.

questions.question: What does Elisha's insistence on mercy reveal about his character and God's nature?

options.A: Mercy triumphs over vengeance; God desires reconciliation and life, not endless cycles of violence

questions.explanation: Elisha embodied God's preference for grace over retribution.

questions.question: How did the king of Israel's obedience to Elisha's counsel produce political results?

options.B: Acts of mercy can transform enemies; the king's kindness ended Syrian raids, creating peace

questions.explanation: Mercy achieved what military victories could not: peace.

questions.question: Why was the king's captain skeptical of Elisha's prophecy?

options.C: The famine was severe, and such sudden abundance seemed impossible even with divine help; human logic doubts miraculous intervention

questions.explanation: The captain lacked faith in God's power to provide.

questions.question: Why did the lepers' position outside the city make them the instruments of deliverance?

options.A: Rejected by society, the lepers were free to go to the Syrian camp; their marginalized status became the means through which they discovered and shared God's provision

questions.explanation: The lepers' outsider status allowed them to access and discover the miracle.

questions.question: How did God's supernatural intervention work without direct violence?

options.B: God used psychological means—fear and confusion—to accomplish His will; divine action transcends purely physical or military solutions

questions.explanation: God's methods demonstrated His wisdom and power beyond martial means.

questions.question: Why did the lepers' decision to report their discovery to the city authorities show growing moral consciousness?

options.A: They moved from self-interest to community concern; having received blessing, they chose to share it rather than hoard it

questions.explanation: The lepers' generosity reflected spiritual transformation.

questions.question: Why did the borrowed axe head carry symbolic importance beyond its practical value?

options.B: The borrowed item represented the young prophet's responsibility and dependence on others; Elisha's concern for this small matter showed God's care for those in need and under obligation

questions.explanation: The incident illustrated how God honors accountability and cares for burdens.

questions.question: How did the king of Syria's attempt to locate and capture Elisha illustrate a fundamental spiritual principle?

options.A: Political power can always overcome spiritual power
options.C: Those who seek to combat God's purposes through human means inevitably fail; spiritual reality cannot be defeated by carnal weapons

questions.explanation: The king's efforts proved futile against God's protective power.

questions.question: Why does the prayer for spiritual perception rank among the most important prayers in scripture?

options.A: To see God's reality is foundational to faith; all true obedience flows from perceiving God's present power and protection
options.D: Only some people need this prayer

questions.explanation: Spiritual sight transforms fear into faith.

questions.question: How did Elisha's command for mercy reflect a higher wisdom than military might?

options.B: True power lies in restraint and grace; the strongest act is often choosing not to destroy but to redeem, breaking cycles of violence through compassion

questions.explanation: Elisha's mercy demonstrated transcendent wisdom.

questions.question: Why did the captain's doubt lead to his personal judgment even as others received blessing?

options.C: Those who reject God's word miss His blessing; the captain's disbelief in the promise resulted in his death at the gate while others ate

questions.explanation: Unbelief separates us from the blessings God offers.

questions.question: Why were the lepers, traditionally seen as cursed, placed in the role of saviors?

options.B: God often uses the marginalized and rejected to accomplish His purposes; the lepers' discovery and sharing of God's provision reversed their social status through their faithfulness

questions.explanation: God elevated the despised to become deliverers.

questions.question: How did God's creation of supernatural sound demonstrate His power over natural and psychological reality?

options.A: God transcends all domains; He can operate through perception and psychology as effectively as through physical force, proving His absolute sovereignty

questions.explanation: The supernatural noise revealed God's dominion over all reality.

questions.question: Why did the lepers' choice to share their discovery represent a key theme of the kingdom of God?

options.C: Kingdom values prioritize community blessing over individual advantage; those who have received grace are called to extend it, breaking cycles of self-interest through generosity

questions.explanation: The lepers embodied kingdom priorities of sharing blessing.

questions.question: Why did Elisha's confidence in God's provision prove warranted despite the king's despair?

options.A: Elisha's faith rested on knowledge of God's character and purposes, which are unshaken by circumstances; faith perceives what faith cannot see

questions.explanation: Elisha's faith was grounded in God's faithfulness, not circumstances.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell the Shunammite woman to do?

options.B: Arise and go with her household, for the LORD hath called for a famine

questions.explanation: Elisha warned her of a coming famine and told her to leave.

questions.question: Who did the king of Syria send to Elisha to inquire if he would recover?
questions.explanation: Hazael was sent with gifts to ask if the king would recover from his illness.

questions.question: What did Elisha tell Hazael?

options.A: The king will surely die, and you will become king
options.D: There is no hope for recovery

questions.explanation: Elisha prophesied that Hazael would become king and persecute Israel.

questions.question: What did Elisha command one of the sons of the prophets to do?

options.B: Go to Ramoth-gilead and anoint Jehu as king

questions.explanation: Elisha sent a prophet to anoint Jehu as Israel's next king.

questions.question: What was done to Jehu?

options.C: He was anointed as king of Israel

questions.explanation: The prophet anointed Jehu with oil as a sign of his kingship.

questions.question: What was Jehu called to do as the new king?

options.B: Avenge the blood of the prophets of the LORD

questions.explanation: Jehu was to execute judgment on Ahab's house for their wickedness.

questions.question: Who fell mortally wounded at Jezreel?
questions.explanation: Jehu shot King Jehoram with an arrow at Jezreel.

questions.question: What did Jezebel do when she saw Jehu coming?

options.A: She painted her eyes, tired her head, and looked out of a window

questions.explanation: Jezebel prepared herself before confronting Jehu.

questions.question: What was Jezebel's fate?

options.B: She was thrown down from the window and trampled by horses

questions.explanation: Jezebel died a violent death, fulfilling Elijah's prophecy.

questions.question: Why was Jehu's anointing as king the fulfillment of earlier prophecies?

options.A: He had no connection to previous events
options.C: Elijah had prophesied judgment on Ahab's house; Jehu became the instrument of that judgment decades later

questions.explanation: Jehu's rise fulfilled the word spoken through Elijah long before.
